summary: Both changes are localized robustness fixes with no regression to existing callers. safeReaddir adding ENOTDIR to its swallowed-error set is safe: all three call sites are best-effort directory enumerations where 'no entries' is correct, and permission/other errors still throw so no actionable failure is masked. The buildEvidenceItem spread-order flip is behavior-preserving because no caller passes a colliding kind/ref in extra, and the four defaulted fields resolve identically via extra.X ?? default; descriptive extra fields are still carried through. Dropping the {...extra, kind} workaround in classifySeniorAxisEvidence is consistent with the new explicit-arg-wins guarantee. All 29 tests across the four named files pass.

inspection.summary: Inspected architecture doc, spec md, spec vibepro.json, the diff vs origin/main for src/execution-state.js and src/pr-manager.js, all buildEvidenceItem call sites and their extra bags, and all three safeReaddir call sites (pr dir/audit root/story-doc dirs). Ran node --test over gate-evidence-edge-robustness + e2e + vibepro-gate-check + adjudication (29 tests, all pass).
inputs: src/pr-manager.js buildEvidenceItem call sites (14 callers, none pass kind/ref in extra)

judgment_delta: Initial concerns: (a) could ENOTDIR-swallowing hide a real error; (b) could the spread-order flip alter existing evidence item fields. Reading safeReaddir call sites confirmed pure enumerations where empty is correct and non-ENOENT/ENOTDIR errors still throw → (a) low risk. Grepping every buildEvidenceItem caller confirmed none rely on extra overriding kind/ref and the ?? default pattern preserves prior resolution → (b) no behavioral change. Passing suite corroborates. Concern → pass.
